title: -Contract No. 32100053 Amendment: Orsolya Kuti, DVM to Provide On-site Veterinary Services for Berkeley Animal Care Services

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution ratifying action taken by the City Manager during recess to amend
Contract No. 32100053 with Orsolya Kuti for on-site veterinary services for Berkeley
Animal Care Services (BACS), increasing the contract amount by $45,000 for a new
total not to exceed amount of $273,000, and extending the contract term to June 30,
2028.
NEGATIVE EFFECT IF ACTION IS DELAYED UNTIL AFTER COUNCIL RECESS
Berkeley Animal Care Services does not have a full-time staff veterinarian and critical
veterinary services at the shelter are provided through this contract. Veterinary
expenses have risen sharply since the pandemic, up 40% since 2020. Amending this
contract to bring it in line with market costs is critical if BACS is to retain this
veterinarian’s services into the future.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Ratifying this contract will result in a total increase in NTE of $45,000. In FY 2026
$9,000 is available in the Animal Care Services General Fund budget (011-21-203-000-
0000-000-424-612410). The remaining amount of the NTE will be appropriated in the
FY 2027 and FY 2028 biennial budget.
sections.current_situation:
On September 1, 2020, the City of Berkeley entered into a three-year contract ending
August 31, 2023, with Orsolya Kuti, DVM to provide on-site veterinary care at the Dona
Spring Animal Shelter. The contract was amended and extended on September 13,
2022, increasing the hourly rate to the current rate of $85/hr. Incorporating significant
annual increases to veterinary expenses over the past several years into the hourly
rate, as described below, results in an adjusted rate of $105/hr.
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 4
Contract Amendment: Orsolya Kuti, DVM for On-site veterinary services CONSENT CALENDAR
January 20, 2026
sections.background:
BACS is a full-service municipal animal care and control agency, enforcing local and
state laws throughout the City of Berkeley (COB) and the City of Albany. BACS is also
contracted to provide shelter and field services for the City of Emeryville, and shelter
services for the City of Piedmont. BACS is an open-door facility which experiences a
constant influx of animals that need to receive affordable veterinary care, treatment, and
spay/neuter services. The BACS shelter is open to the public seven days a week to
receive stray, surrendered and confiscated animals. Additionally, field services are
provided seven days a week, during which Animal Control Officers impound stray,
surrendered and confiscated animals. Many animals arrive at the shelter sick or injured
and in need of immediate veterinary care. On-site veterinary services include but are
not limited to:
 Examining and diagnosing shelter animals,
 Treating sick or injured animals,
 Performing spay/neuter surgeries, and
 Performing other routine surgeries.
To ensure that BACS euthanasia rate remains below the national benchmark of 10%,
sick or injured animals must obtain necessary treatment so that they have a much better
chance of rescue or adoption.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects or climate impacts associated with the
subject of this report.
sections.rationale:
Dr. Kuti has been a practicing veterinarian since 2017. Her first contract with BACS was
signed on July 15, 2020, at an hourly rate of $75. She received her only raise during her
five years with BACS on September 14, 2022, when her hourly rate was increased to
$85. Throughout this time, she has taken significantly less money to work at BACS.
Meanwhile, the cost of veterinary care has increased 40% since 20201, This averages
to an annual rise of around 8%, translating to a 24% increase since the last adjustment
in 2022. Applying this increase since Dr. Kuti's last contract adjustment results in a $105
hourly cost. This is commensurate with Bay Area veterinary costs and remains
significantly lower than those in comparable positions.
1 See: Why are veterinarian bills getting so ruff on the wallet? : The Indicator from Planet Money : NPR
Page 2
Page 3 of 4
Contract Amendment: Orsolya Kuti, DVM for On-site veterinary services CONSENT CALENDAR
January 20, 2026
sections.alternatives:
Failure to bring Dr. Kuti’s contract to a rate that is more consistent with current market
pricing risks a loss of her services altogether. Were this to happen, BACS would be
forced to outsource surgeries (which will result in significant and unavoidable cost
increases to the organization of up to an estimated $90,000 annually) or to find a relief
vet (again resulting in unavoidable cost increases of up to an estimated $18,000
annually).
CONTACT PERSON
Mike St Pierre, Manager, Berkeley Animal Care Services, (510) 981-6603.
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 3
Page 4 of 4
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 32100053 AMENDMENT: ORSOLYA KUTI, DVM TO PROVIDE ON-
SITE VETERINARY SERVICES FOR BERKELEY ANIMAL CARE SERVICES (BACS)
WHEREAS, BACS has a constant influx of animals that need to receive immediate and
affordable veterinary care and treatment; and
WHEREAS, sick or injured animals that obtain necessary procedures and/or treatment/s
have a much better chance of rescue or adoption; and
WHEREAS, Dr. Orsolya Kuti is a highly qualified veterinarian with extensive experience
and expertise in shelter medicine, and has been providing on-site veterinary services to
BACS since 2020; and
WHEREAS, the costs of veterinary expenses have risen significantly in the past several
years, necessitating an adjustment to Dr. Kuti’s hourly rate to remain competitive with
comparable market pricing; and
WHEREAS, the contract will be funded as a General Fund allocation, the full funding for
which will be subject to the City’s FY27-28 Biennial Budget adoption and annual
appropriation process.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
Council ratifies the action taken by the City Manager to execute an amendment to
Contract No. 32100053 with Orsolya Kuti for on-site veterinary services for Berkeley
Animal Care Services (BACS), increasing the contract amount by $45,000 for a new total
not to exceed amount of $273,000, and extending the contract term to June 30, 2028.
